## Highlights

- **SPEC §4.5 QR-import binding check now enforced + visible** (catching iOS up to `android-v1.2.18`/`v1.2.19`) — scanning an identity card whose `destHash` doesn't match `SHA-256(name_hash ‖ identity_hash)` of the embedded `publicKey` is now refused outright instead of silently overwriting the announce-verified row. The previous `applyIdentityCard` fall back to `lxmf.delivery` whenever `inferServiceType` returned null accepted forged cards where the destHash was a victim's real address but the publicKey was the attacker's — every subsequent outgoing opportunistic LXMF would then encrypt to the attacker, and genuine Alice announces would be refused under the existing first-announcer-wins check. The shared engine now treats a null inference as a hard reject and mirrors `handleAnnounce`'s first-announcer-wins refusal, so the QR-import surface isn't a side door around the announce-path check.
- **QR rejection is now visible.** Pre-`v1.0.81` the refusal only landed in `lastConnectError`, which renders solely in Settings → TCP transport — invisible to anyone scanning from the Nodes tab. A new `.alert("QR import rejected", ...)` on `NodesView` fires immediately with the §4.5 reason text so a forged-card refusal can't be mistaken for silent success.
